name: Iran Nuclear Program & HEU Custody Architecture
summary: 2026-06-10 (Wednesday; Day 103; covers 7 June cutoff -> 8-9 June -> early 10 June; June 8 & 9 pipeline runs empty, deltas vs the June 7 brief): **THE WAR'S SHARPEST ESCALATION TOUCHED THE BALLISTIC-MISSILE MATERIALS SUPPLY CHAIN FOR THE FIRST TIME, BUT THE NUCLEAR/HEU FILE STAYED UNCHANGED.** Israel's 8-June deep strike into Iran hit the Mahshahr petrochemical complex, which the IDF said 'produces unique materials critical for Iran's ballistic-missile program' — the first strike this window touching the missile-materials supply chain (a missile-MATERIALS, not an enrichment, target). The HEU file is unchanged: no enrichment-resumption confirmed and no walk-back of the reported Mojtaba directive (~440kg 60%-HEU not to leave Iran) across the escalation. Trump's nuclear-letter accept/reject deadline is 15 June — 5 days out, with NO formal Iranian response and talks in 'reassessment' (Baghaei) -> EXPIRY-UNANSWERED trajectory. No trigger moves to active — no confirmed enrichment resumption or HEU movement. (intel A5, nuclear-program node) 2026-06-07 (Sunday): **THE NUCLEAR FILE STAYED DEFERRED WHILE THE HEU-DIRECTIVE WINDOW EXPIRED AND THE IMPASSE MOVED FULLY ONTO MONEY.** The reported Mojtaba directive that Iran's ~440kg of 60%-enriched uranium not leave the country remains un-walked-back, but the iran/mojtaba-node trigger demanding state-media confirmation OR a walk-back within 14 days (by 2026-06-05) EXPIRED WITH NEITHER — the thread went dark exactly on schedule, a weak signal the directive is internal posture not an operational act (intel A5). Substantively the deadlock has shifted from the nuclear file to MONEY (the $24bn — see iran/united-states B1); Secretary Rubio reframed HEU disposal as a later 'phase two' of negotiations. The IAEA continues to report a complete LOSS OF CONTINUITY on the stockpile (access terminated 28 Feb), with Grossi's standing line that Iran 'isn't actively enriching' but that 'movement [was] detected near the stockpile.' Iran's non-response is the operative reality. (Russia-HEU-custodian trigger, by 2026-06-11, still watching.) Day 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5 June -> 5-6 June overnight Gulf exchange). **THE DEADLOCK IS NOW MONEY, NOT NUCLEAR — THE HEU DIRECTIVE STAYED UN-WALKED-BACK, THE ~5-JUNE WINDOW PASSED QUIETLY, AND THE IAEA KEPT REPORTING LOSS OF CONTINUITY PLUS 'MOVEMENT DETECTED NEAR THE STOCKPILE.'** The genuinely new framing (intel A2/A5): Rezaei — military adviser to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei — told CNN (5 June) the negotiations are 'at a deadlock' over $24bn in frozen Iranian assets ('the ball is in Trump's court,' a doubling from $12bn), making explicit that the binding sticking-point is now MONEY, not the nuclear file — which Iran continues to defer. The reported Mojtaba directive that Iran's ~440kg of 60%-enriched uranium NOT leave the country remains UN-WALKED-BACK (REPORTED, single-lineage / two Iranian sources), with no proof-of-life, and the internal ~5-June window has now PASSED QUIETLY — consistent with internal posture, not an operational act (~200kg reportedly in the Isfahan tunnel complex). The IAEA continues to report a complete LOSS OF CONTINUITY on the pre-war 60% stockpile (access terminated 28 Feb — cameras off, seals removed); Grossi says Iran 'isn't actively enriching but movement detected near the stockpile,' and urged Iran to engage 'without delay.' Iran's non-response — the verification blackout — is the operative reality. No trigger moves to active — the IAEA observation is a continuity report, not a confirmed enrichment-resumption or kinetic event; the deadlock reframing is stated-position. PRIOR — Day 97 (Thursday June 4; covers 3 June afternoon -> 4 June US day / 5 June IST). **THE IAEA WENT PUBLIC ON THE HEU FILE (CONFIRMED) WHILE IRAN SOFTENED THE CHANNEL BUT KEPT NUCLEAR FROZEN — THE FORUM IS UNFROZEN AT THE MULTILATERAL LEVEL, NOT THE BILATERAL.** The genuinely new element: the IAEA on 4 June formally urged Tehran to engage 'constructively' and allow verification 'without delay,' acknowledging the strikes created 'an unprecedented situation' (CONFIRMED — RFE/RL) — the multilateral body asserting itself on the nuclear file as the bilateral track stalls, and giving the ~440.9 kg / 60% stockpile question a forum independent of Washington. But Iran's non-response (the verification blackout continues since 28 Feb — cameras off, seals removed, the longest on record) is the operative reality. On the bilateral track, Iran SOFTENED the channel language ('communications not cut off, messages exchanged') but reported 'no tangible progress' and kept nuclear off-table behind the Beirut precondition (intel A2). The Mojtaba HEU-retention directive remains un-confirmed/un-walked-back (~at/just past the ~5 June window; ~200kg reportedly in the Isfahan tunnel complex). No trigger moves to active — the IAEA call is a diplomatic action, not a verification event. PRIOR — Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers 2 June afternoon -> 3 June morning). **THE NUCLEAR LIMB MOVED FURTHER OUT OF REACH — IRAN HARDENED THE SUSPENSION ('NO RESPONSE GIVEN, TEXT-EXCHANGE HALTED') WHILE RUBIO INSISTED THE NUCLEAR FILE IS STILL IN PLAY.** Iran's 3 June hardening (Tasnim: 'no response given on the text of the understanding,' text-exchange suspended through intermediaries until Lebanon conditions are met) removes the very forum in which HEU disposal/custodian arrangements would be negotiated — the nuclear limb is structurally harder, not closer. Against it, Rubio told Senate Foreign Relations there is 'the prospect' Iran 'could negotiate aspects of their nuclear programme' (CLAIMED, optimistic bound). The Mojtaba Khamenei directive that Iran's ~440kg of 60% HEU not leave the country remains un-confirmed and un-walked-back, ~Day 13 of 14 (deadline ~5 June, ~48h out), still no verified proof-of-life; ~200kg reportedly in the Isfahan tunnel complex. The binary for ~5 June is unchanged: does state media ever confirm or quietly drop the directive. No trigger moves to active. PRIOR — Day 95 (Monday June 2; covers 30 May afternoon -> 2 June morning). **THE NUCLEAR LIMB IS UNCHANGED-HARD AND NOW FURTHER OUT OF REACH — IRAN SUSPENDED THE TALKS AND KEPT NUCLEAR OFF THE TABLE. With Iran's 1 June suspension of the indirect talks (intel A1) and IRNA framing this round as 'cessation-of-hostilities only,' the suspension removes the very forum in which HEU disposal would be negotiated — the nuclear limb is now structurally harder to resolve, not closer. The Mojtaba Khamenei directive that Iran's enriched uranium not leave the country remains un-confirmed and un-walked-back — ~Day 12 of the 14-day window (deadline ~5 June), still no verified proof-of-life. Al Jazeera quantifies the stockpile at ~440kg / 970lb of 60%-enriched uranium — short of the 90% weapons-grade threshold but the fast-breakout point and central deal sticking-point. One 2 June aggregation floated that the 'new supreme leader may be engaged with US talks' — unverified, soft, contradicted by the suspension; NOT elevated. The binary for ~5 June is unchanged: does state media ever confirm or quietly drop the directive. No trigger moves to active. PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30). **THE NUCLEAR LIMB GOT HARDER, NOT CLOSER — IRAN PUBLICLY DENIES NEGOTIATIONS ARE EVEN HAPPENING AND REPRICES ITS ASK AS CASH. On 29 May Iran publicly claimed 'no negotiations' are taking place on its nuclear program (CLAIMED Iran), directly countering Trump's 'close to finalizing with strong inspections.' Araghchi reaffirmed a 'deadlock' on enriched material, with the topic 'postponed' to later stages (REPORTED — at the BRICS FM meeting). The genuinely new intelligence is Iran's MONEY-FIRST reframing: Fars (semi-official) asserts the unsigned 60-day MOU draft contains NO reference to dismantling/destroying nuclear material and that the deal's 'most important part' is the immediate $12bn frozen-asset release (REPORTED — Iran's framing, contested). Trump's counter-claim that the ~1,000 lbs HEU 'will be destroyed' is CLAIMED — US framing, with no signed text. The Mojtaba Khamenei directive that HEU not leave Iran stands un-walked-back; no proof-of-life surfaced — the directive watch is ~Day 9 of 14 (deadline ~June 5). Treat both the destruction clause (US) and the $12bn-first demand (Iran) as CLAIMED until a signed text exists. NO trigger moves.** Updated 2026-05-28 (Day 90 of Hormuz blockade). **THE NUCLEAR FILE HIT 'DEADLOCK' — THE CLEANEST NEW NEGATIVE OF THE DAY. FM Araghchi declared the two sides had reached a 'DEADLOCK' on enriched uranium, with that file 'postponed' to later stages (REPORTED). Against that, Trump hardened on the record (CONFIRMED — PBS, CNBC): Iran's ~1,000 lbs of HEU 'will be destroyed' — in the US, in Iran, or 'at another acceptable location' — and he OPPOSES Russia OR China taking custody ('No dust, no dollars'); 'No sanctions, no money, no nothing.' Khamenei adviser Shamkhani again called US control of Iran's nuclear program a 'fantasy.' The White House dismissed an Iranian-state-TV draft framework (gradual Oman-mediated Hormuz reopening, US withdrawal, blockade lifting) as a 'complete fabrication' (B1, CLAIMED vs DENIED). Tasnim simultaneously claimed the Doha nuclear-stockpile talks were 'generally positive' — directly contradicting Araghchi's 'deadlock' (a within-regime mixed signal). The Mojtaba HEU-retention directive is now Day 7 of the 14-day window (by June 5) — state media STILL neither confirms nor walks it back; the negative-space signal persists. NET: the disposal-vs-retention contradiction has HARDENED INTO AN EXPLICIT DEADLOCK, and the third-party-custodian path (Russia OR China) is now affirmatively foreclosed on the US side. NO upgrade on REPORTED/CLAIMED-CONTESTED inputs; today is rhetoric/diplomatic posture + a negotiating-state report, not confirmed nuclear action.** PRIOR (May 27): Day 89 of Hormuz blockade; ~48h coverage. **THE DEAL REPORTEDLY INCLUDES AN HEU 'DISPOSAL' MECHANISM — YET SHAMKHANI CALLED US CONTROL OF IRAN'S NUCLEAR PROGRAMME A 'FANTASY' AND IRAN WANTS NUCLEAR DEFERRED. Trump's 'largely negotiated' deal reportedly contains a mechanism to 'dispose of' Iran's HEU stockpile and 'strong inspections'; but Khamenei adviser Ali Shamkhani dismissed Trump's desired control over Iran's nuclear programme as a 'fantasy,' Iran insists the nuclear file be deferred to the 60-day window, and US reporting still lists the nuclear file as one of three deal-blockers (alongside sanctions relief and Lebanon). The Mojtaba HEU-retention directive is now Day 6 of the 14-day window (by June 5) — and Iranian state media has STILL neither confirmed nor walked it back; the negative-space signal persists. The three-way contradiction is unchanged: admin deal-optimist (disposal agreed) vs Iran nuclear-preservation (deferred / 'fantasy' on control, directive un-walked) vs Trump-Netanyahu private maximalist (dismantlement required).** **NO upgrade on REPORTED/CLAIMED-CONTESTED inputs; today is rhetoric/diplomatic posture, not confirmed nuclear action.** PRIOR (May 25): **THE NUCLEAR TRACK'S CORE CONTRADICTION SHARPENED AND PERSISTED: US SOURCES (AXIOS) SAY IRANIANS GAVE ASSURANCES 'VERBAL AND IN WRITING' THAT ANY PERMANENT DEAL WILL INCLUDE THE 'DISPOSAL' OF ALL ENRICHED URANIUM (HIGHER AND LOWER LEVELS), AND THAT MOJTABA KHAMENEI 'ENDORSED THE BROAD TEMPLATE' (FINAL APPROVAL OPEN) — AGAINST IRAN FM BAGHAEI MAY 25: NUCLEAR 'ISN'T PART OF DISCUSSIONS AT THIS STAGE.' IRANIAN STATE MEDIA HAS STILL NEITHER CONFIRMED NOR WALKED BACK THE MOJTABA HEU-RETENTION DIRECTIVE — DAY 4 OF THE 14-DAY WINDOW (BY JUNE 5); THE NEGATIVE-SPACE SIGNAL PERSISTS. PEZESHKIAN SAID IRAN 'IS READY TO ASSURE THE WORLD IT IS NOT PURSUING NUCLEAR WEAPONS' — A NON-PROLIFERATION ASSURANCE COMPATIBLE WITH BOTH KEEPING AND DISPOSING OF THE STOCKPILE, RESOLVING NOTHING.** (1) **US disposal-assurance claim (REPORTED — Axios May 24, single channel)**: 'verbal and in writing' assurances that a subsequent permanent deal will include 'disposal' of all enriched uranium; Mojtaba 'endorsed the broad template.' (2) **Iran position (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — Baghaei via NBC/CNBC/IRNA/Press TV May 25)**: 'nuclear isn't part of discussions at this stage'; the Doha delegation's agenda explicitly includes 'highly enriched uranium' but on Iran's procedural terms, with details deferred. (3) **Mojtaba HEU-retention directive Day 4 of 14**: NO Iranian state-media confirmation, denial, or walk-back; the negative-space signal continues. (4) **Pezeshkian no-weapons assurance (CONFIRMED RHETORIC)**: rhetorically compatible with both keeping and disposing — resolves the disposal-vs-retention contradiction in neither direction. (5) **Doha money/venue track (context)**: a senior Iranian delegation (Ghalibaf, Araghchi, central-bank governor Hemmati) is in Doha working Hormuz + HEU + frozen-asset unfreezing with Qatar's PM — the HEU workstream now has a physical venue but no published text. **The three-way contradiction is unchanged and unresolved**: admin deal-optimist (disposal agreed, verbal+written) vs Iran nuclear-preservation (not in current talks, directive un-walked) vs Trump-Netanyahu private maximalist (dismantlement required). **Falsifiable**: a published MOU within 14 days resolves it; explicit Iranian state-media confirmation OR walk-back of the Mojtaba directive within the window (by June 5) falsifies one framing. Sadjadpour asymmetric-split thesis intact — Iran appears to be getting the ceasefire/Hormuz/sanctions-without-uranium-dismantlement split it wanted, but only inside the deferred-window architecture. **NO upgrade on REPORTED/CLAIMED-CONTESTED inputs; today is rhetoric/diplomatic posture, not confirmed nuclear action.** PRIOR (May 24): **THE NUCLEAR TRACK IS NOW THE CLEANEST CONTRADICTION IN THE DEAL ARCHITECTURE: CBS SENIOR ADMIN SOURCE 'IRANIANS HAVE IN PRINCIPLE AGREED TO A DEAL THAT WOULD INCLUDE THE DISPOSAL OF HIGHLY ENRICHED URANIUM' vs IRAN FM BAGHAEI 'NOT YET ACCEPTED ANY ACTIONS ON ITS NUCLEAR PROGRAMME' AND 'NUCLEAR ISSUES ARE NOT A FOCUS OF THE PEACE TALKS AT THIS STAGE.' TRUMP-NETANYAHU PRIVATE CALL: TRUMP 'WILL NOT SIGN ANY FINAL AGREEMENT WITHOUT THE CONDITIONS THAT IRAN DISMANTLE ITS ENTIRE NUCLEAR PROGRAM AND REMOVE ALL ENRICHED URANIUM' — DIRECTLY CONTRADICTS US ADMIN PUBLIC 'IN PRINCIPLE AGREED TO DISPOSAL.' MOJTABA URANIUM DIRECTIVE DAY 3 OF 14 — NO IRANIAN STATE-MEDIA CONFIRMATION OR WALK-BACK. THE NUCLEAR TRACK HAS BIFURCATED INTO THREE COMPETING NARRATIVES: ADMIN DEAL-OPTIMIST (DISPOSAL AGREED) vs IRAN NUCLEAR-PRESERVATION (NOT IN MOU) vs TRUMP-NETANYAHU PRIVATE MAXIMALIST (DISMANTLEMENT REQUIRED).** (1) **CBS senior administration source (REPORTED — single anonymous channel)**: 'the Iranians have in principle agreed to a deal that would include the disposal of highly enriched uranium.' (2) **Iran FM Baghaei (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — IRNA, Press TV, Mehr, Jerusalem Post)**: Iran has 'not yet accepted any actions on its nuclear programme'; 'nuclear issues are not a focus of the peace talks at this stage'; 'a conclusion would not be reached if the US sought to delve into details about highly enriched uranium in Iran.' (3) **Trump-Netanyahu private call (REPORTED — TOI sources)**: Trump assured Netanyahu he 'will not sign any final agreement without the conditions that Iran dismantle its entire nuclear program and remove all enriched uranium from the country.' (4) **Axios 60-day MOU architecture (REPORTED)** on nuclear track: Iran commits never to pursue weapons + negotiate enrichment suspension + negotiate removal of HEU stockpile, with details deferred to the 60-day window — NOT immediate dismantlement, NOT immediate removal. (5) **Mojtaba directive Day 3 of 14**: no Iranian state-media confirmation/denial/walk-back. Baghaei's framing is implicit alignment with directive substance without attribution. (6) **Netanyahu publicly: 'position remained unchanged: Iran will not have nuclear weapons.'** (7) **The three-way contradiction is the structural signal**: either (a) Trump is publicly double-tracking (telling Netanyahu privately the maximalist line, telling the press the moderate line, accepting Iran's actual procedural floor); (b) the admin source over-stated Iranian acceptance to drive deal momentum; (c) the deal text has deliberately ambiguous language that allows each side to claim its version. **Falsifiable**: published MOU text within 14 days will resolve the contradiction one way or the other; explicit Iranian state-media denial of disposal-agreement within 7 days fully falsifies the admin source. **Sadjadpour asymmetric-split thesis at maximum institutional expression** — Iran appears to be getting the asymmetric split it wanted (ceasefire/Hormuz/sanctions deliverable without uranium dismantlement) but only inside the 60-day window architecture. PRIOR (May 23): NUCLEAR-TRACK BIFURCATION OPERATIONALLY EXPLICIT IN TRUMP'S OWN LANGUAGE MAY 23: URANIUM MUST BE 'SATISFACTORILY HANDLED' (vs MAY 16 LETTER 'FULL DISMANTLEMENT') — VERBAL ACCOMMODATION OF MOJTABA DIRECTIVE'S SUBSTANCE WITHOUT FORMAL DEAL. MOJTABA DIRECTIVE DAY 2 OF 14 (NO IRANIAN STATE-MEDIA CONFIRMATION OR WALK-BACK). IRAN FM BAGHAEI: 'A CONCLUSION WOULD NOT BE REACHED IF THE US SOUGHT TO DELVE INTO DETAILS ABOUT HIGHLY ENRICHED URANIUM IN IRAN.'** (1) **Trump May 23 framing** (CBS primary): two non-negotiables — Iran cannot obtain nuclear weapons; enriched uranium must be 'satisfactorily handled.' This is a MEANINGFUL SOFTENING from May 16 letter's 'full dismantling + zero enrichment within 2 months.' (2) **Iran FM Baghaei publicly framed deal timeline as '30-60 days' for finalizing MOU details** (Al Jazeera May 23, IRNA) BUT explicitly noted nuclear issues are 'not being discussed' at the current stage; 'a conclusion would not be reached if the US sought to delve into details about highly enriched uranium in Iran' — structurally consistent with Mojtaba directive's substance without explicit attribution. (3) **Mojtaba directive Day 2 of 14**: no Iranian state-media outlet (IRNA, Tasnim, Mehr, Press TV) has confirmed, denied, or walked back the May 21 leaked directive. Daily Sabah May 22, Geo TV, Al Arabiya, Kathmandu Post all recycle the original Reuters wire without independent sourcing. (4) **The asymmetric-split deal architecture Iran appears to want is now in Trump's own verbal posture**: ceasefire/Hormuz/sanctions track is what Trump is talking about publicly ('every day better'); nuclear track is what he has quietly softened on ('satisfactorily handled'). **Vali Nasr 'time on Iran's side' thesis operationally validated**: Iran did not have to publicly concede the nuclear track and the US president has verbally accommodated the directive's substance. (5) **Falsifiable updated**: deal deliverable within 7 days addressing Hormuz/ceasefire/sanctions WITHOUT uranium dismantlement = Sadjadpour asymmetric-split thesis confirmed; Trump pivots back to May 16 letter terms within 7 days = thesis partially falsified. PRIOR (May 22): **MOJTABA KHAMENEI'S MAY 21 LEAKED DIRECTIVE — URANIUM ENRICHED TO 60% TO 'REMAIN STRICTLY INSIDE IRANIAN TERRITORY' — IS THE CLEANEST PUBLIC POLICY FORECLOSURE OF THE NUCLEAR DEAL TRACK OF THE CYCLE.** Anonymous Iranian sources told Reuters (propagated via Algemeiner / Kathmandu Post / CDM Press May 21) that 'the Supreme Leader's directive, and the consensus within the establishment, is that the stockpile of enriched uranium should not leave the country.' Reasoning per sources: Iran would be 'more vulnerable to future attacks by the United States and Israel' if material left. **The directive directly contradicts Trump's May 16 letter, which required full dismantlement + zero enrichment within 2 months.** The leaked Al Arabiya text (May 22) — Pakistan-mediated draft addressing Hormuz/ceasefire/sanctions — OMITS uranium dismantlement entirely. **The deal architecture has bifurcated**: a ceasefire/Hormuz/sanctions deliverable is executable on the operational track via Pakistan; the nuclear deliverable is foreclosed by Mojtaba's directive. **Sadjadpour falsifiable**: if a deal is publicly announced inside 7 days addressing Hormuz/ceasefire/sanctions WITHOUT uranium dismantlement = thesis confirmed (regime accepted asymmetric split). If Trump walks away citing nuclear-track failure = thesis confirmed (asymmetric split was deliberate). If Iran softens the Mojtaba directive within 14 days = thesis wrong (directive was bargaining noise). **Verification**: Reuters wire via 3 outlets traces to anonymous Iranian sources — counts as ONE source. Tag at REPORTED, not CONFIRMED. **The Issacharoff thesis (May 18) is structurally strengthened by the directive**: Iran's nuclear infrastructure remains largely intact underground; Tehran is signaling it has NOT, and will NOT, convert the nuclear program into deal currency. **Rubio's May 21 Hormuz red line (deal 'unfeasible' if Iran continues pursuing toll mechanism)** sets a SECOND failure mode parallel to the nuclear-track failure mode — even if Iran walks back the uranium directive, the Iran-Oman toll mechanism is a separate publicly-stated US-acceptability test. PRIOR (May 19): **HAARETZ MAY 18 — 'IRAN'S NUCLEAR INFRASTRUCTURE AND MISSILE PRODUCTION CAPABILITIES REMAIN LARGELY INTACT UNDERGROUND' — A FORMER SENIOR ISRAELI INTELLIGENCE OFFICER FRAMES IRAN AS NOW MORE, NOT LESS, LIKELY TO WEAPONIZE.** Cleanest publicly stated Israeli-intelligence case that the strikes-so-far produced weaponization incentive rather than dismantlement. Officer warns publicly through Haaretz that **Tehran may now conclude that only nuclear weapons can deter future wars** — structural argument that frames Iran's strategic incentive to push for weaponization regardless of the immediate kinetic outcome. **If accurate, kinetic action by Tuesday May 19 NSC could ACCELERATE Iranian weaponization rather than prevent it** — inverts Trump administration's strategic premise. **A11 NYT preparation report stands; Tuesday May 19 Trump NSC meeting is the operational binary**: Day 1 of 6 remaining in NYT 'as soon as next week' window (closes May 24). Trump 'clock is ticking' Truth Social Sunday May 17 + Trump-Netanyahu phone call + Israeli security cabinet meeting. **Iran has not publicly addressed Kharg air-defense reinforcement or NYT preparation report through Monday morning IST** — trigger 'Iran formally confirms or denies Kharg air-defense reinforcement / NYT preparation report' remains UNTRIPPED. **Iran's six-pillar institutional architecture** (Araghchi defer-enrichment + Shamkhani conditional-acceptance + Pezeshkian never-bow + Akraminia surprising-options + IRGC drill closed + Pakistan-Naqvi mediation Day 2 of 14) operationally visible. **Falsifiable tests**: (a) Iranian formal acknowledgement of enrichment to >20% U-235 within 90 days (by 2026-08-18) = Issacharoff-thesis confirms; (b) Iranian formal acceptance of May 16 Trump letter (full dismantling + 2-month compliance) by 2026-06-15 = Issacharoff-thesis fails; (c) Trump signs framework with verification within 30 days (by 2026-06-15) = Nasr structural-mismatch fails; (d) Trump issues public framework deliverable OR actual strikes within 10 days (by 2026-05-27) = Nasr reversal-as-base-case testable. **PRIOR (May 17): MAY 16-17 ESCALATION ON NUCLEAR FILE: KINETIC PREPARATION POSTURE REPORTED FOR THE FIRST TIME IN THE CYCLE.** NYT (Friday May 15 / Saturday May 16, citing two Middle Eastern officials) REPORTED that Israel + US are conducting 'most intense preparations yet to renew attacks on Iran possibly as soon as next week' — options include **(i) commando operation to extract nuclear material believed buried under rubble at struck nuclear sites** ('thousands of supporting forces to create a perimeter,' US officials acknowledged 'would risk several casualties' and likely engage Iranian ground troops); **(ii) ground forces to seize Kharg Island** (handles up to 90% of Iranian oil exports). Amplified by Times of Israel May 16 liveblog, Jerusalem Post 'US-Israel Iran attacks could resume next week' (article 896353), Gulf News 'Operation Epic Fury 2.0,' Fox News 'Next US move on Iran: Seize Kharg Island.' **NO on-record Pentagon/White House confirmation.** Iran has REPORTEDLY (single-source US intel chain via NYT) moved additional military personnel and air defenses to Kharg Island in recent weeks. **The nuclear file is now under direct kinetic-preparation pressure, not just rhetorical pressure** — operationally distinct from any prior episode in the cycle. **Iran's six-pillar institutional architecture (Araghchi defer-enrichment + Shamkhani conditional-acceptance + Pezeshkian never-bow + Akraminia surprising-options + IRGC drill closed + NEW Pakistan-Naqvi mediation) is now operationally visible** — the more pillars, the harder for US to extract a single binding commitment within Trump's 2-month compliance window. **PRIOR (May 16):** A1 TRUMP ON MAY 16 CONFIRMED A NEW NUCLEAR PROPOSAL LETTER TO IRAN demanding swift progress to avoid serious consequences** (CONFIRMED letter EXISTENCE — Trump confirmed May 16; CLAIMED letter CONTENTS — RedState May 15 + Wikipedia 2025-2026 negotiations summary; primary text not yet surfaced). Analysis pieces report the letter demands Iran **fully dismantle its nuclear program, halt all enrichment, and end support for regional proxy groups, with compliance required within two months**. **Khamenei advisor Ali Shamkhani publicly responded that Iran is ready to sign in exchange for quick removal of all financial sanctions** but criticised Trump's rhetoric — cleanest Iranian conditional-acceptance signal of the cycle, attached to the Supreme Leader's office rather than civilian government. **Iranian officials denied receiving any new US proposal and insisted Iran would not give up the right to enrich uranium** (Tasnim/Press TV). **FM Araghchi (Tasnim primary May 15)**: enrichment 'is currently not on the agenda of discussions or negotiations' but 'will be addressed in later stages' — cleanest tactical-defer framing of cycle. **Iran's diplomatic choreography now refined into 5 institutional pillars (Araghchi defer-enrichment, Shamkhani conditional-acceptance, Pezeshkian never-bow, Akraminia surprising-options, IRGC drill 'all objectives achieved') — internally-consistent multi-track posture rather than synchronised hardening.** **The HEU-via-China door yesterday's brief recorded as 'soft-confirmed at the rhetorical-alignment level' is now operationally EMPTY** — the Trump-Xi joint statement WAS the deliverable, not a step toward one. **The Trump 2-month letter window (~2026-07-16 ceiling) is now the binding decision-tree input for the nuclear file** through Q3 2026. **Sadjadpour falsifiable**: if Iran formally accepts May 16 letter terms by 2026-07-16, multi-track-posture-as-leverage reading fails. **Nasr falsifiable**: if Trump signs framework with verification within 30 days (by 2026-06-15), structural-mismatch leg fails. **Helima Croft framework**: 'binary event window' around May 20 is now skewed bearish for paper assets — no operational follow-on materialised in first 48h post-summit. PRIOR (May 14): **A1 TRUMP PUBLICLY OPENED HEU-VIA-CHINA CUSTODIAN DOOR AT TRUMP-XI SUMMIT DAY 1**: 'only Washington or Beijing can retrieve Iran's nuclear material if Tehran agrees to hand it over' (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — Times of Israel May 14 liveblog primary). **MATERIAL POLICY SIGNAL — substitutes China for Russia as proposed HEU custodian on Day 15 of 21 Russia-track window** (US officials had reportedly ruled out Russia-as-custodian per multiple May reports). Framework remains stalled on substance — Iran's HEU return-clause demand and Trump's text-light approach unchanged — but **public custodian door now re-opened in different jurisdiction**. Xi has NOT publicly accepted any custodian role. **B5 IRAN'S AMBASSADOR TO CHINA PUBLICLY FRAMED BEIJING AS 'POLITICAL BALANCING' PARTNER** (Tasnim primary + CBS amplification CONFIRMED RHETORIC): 'China had paved the way for previous direct U.S.-Iran peace talks in Islamabad'; 'predicted Beijing could emerge as a key player in ongoing diplomacy' — institutional ground laid in Iranian state media for exactly the HEU-via-China structure Trump opened. **Diplomatic architecture pivot from Russia-track-stalled to China-track-opening visible from BOTH sides.** Russia structurally sidelined; Pravda silent. PRIOR (May 13): Iranian Parliament National Security and Foreign Policy Commission spokesperson Ebrahim Rezaei said May 12 (Mehr News / Iran International / Tabnak / Euronews / Al Bawaba): '90 percent enrichment may be one of Iran's available options if the country is subjected to another attack' — FIRST TIME IN CYCLE a sitting Iranian parliamentary spokesperson has publicly named weapons-grade enrichment as a retaliatory threshold. Current stated ceiling is 60% with ~440 kg stockpile; 90% line is the breakout-to-bomb threshold. Critically: CONDITIONAL on a future attack, NOT an order or confirmed change to operational policy — but it REFRAMES YESTERDAY'S HEU-WITH-RETURN-CLAUSE CONCESSION: the Parliament is publicly walking up the escalation ladder at the same time the executive is offering custody flexibility through Pakistan. Tasnim ran on-record source: 'No one in Iran drafts plans to please Trump' — rejecting US 'garbage' framing. IRNA carried Pezeshkian 'talks are not surrender.' The Tasnim-IRNA-Parliament axis is now UNIFORMLY DEFIANT rather than divergent (opposite of April pattern when Tasnim was hawkish and IRNA more measured). Iran International (opposition-leaning, Saudi-funded) amplifying Rezaei quote at face value — serves both Iranian-state escalation messaging AND opposition narrative of regime irrationality. Deputy Labour Minister Mohammadi publicly admitted >1 million direct job losses + 2 million unemployment-impacted from war — Tehran is publicly admitting domestic-economic deterioration AND publicly hardening on nuclear file at the SAME TIME. Classical regime-survival externalisation playbook now visible. No US (Trump/WH) response to Rezaei threat as of May 13 morning. Netanyahu 60 Minutes Sunday 'go in and take it out' position structurally aligned with Israeli hawk camp. **THE NUCLEAR FILE REMAINS THE CENTRAL DIPLOMATIC AXIS OF THE CYCLE — but hardening rhetoric narrows landing zone from both ends.** Persistent policy axis covering Iran's nuclear program, enrichment infrastructure, HEU stockpile custody architecture, and the US/Israel/IAEA/Russia inter-state framework around it. **B2 IRAN'S MAY 10-11 COUNTER-PROPOSAL CONTAINED THE FIRST HEU CONCESSION OF THE CYCLE (REPORTED via WSJ primary): dilute portion of HEU stockpile under IAEA supervision + transfer remainder to A THIRD COUNTRY, but with explicit RETURN CLAUSE demanding HEU returnability if US exits any future deal — AND explicitly REJECTING dismantling of enrichment facilities.** The HEU return-clause is the structural sticking point: real concession on PHYSICAL custody but hard floor against permanent commitment. Russia (Putin May 9 standing offer) is the implicit third-country candidate but no party publicly named it. **TRUMP PUBLICLY REJECTED THE COUNTER-PROPOSAL AS 'TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE'** — HEU-transfer-with-return-clause now in rejection state, framework on 'massive life support.' **B5 NETANYAHU CBS 60 MINUTES MAY 11: war 'NOT OVER' until URANIUM REMOVED + ENRICHMENT SITES DISMANTLED + PROXIES NEUTRALIZED — 'YOU GO IN AND YOU TAKE IT OUT.'** Haaretz framed Netanyahu's uranium-removal demand as 'his lifeline' = domestic political survival, not strategic necessity. **B7 NEW OFAC SANCTIONS WAVE MAY 11-12 INCLUDED IRANIAN NUCLEAR RESEARCH WITH POSSIBLE MILITARY APPLICATIONS.** **THE NUCLEAR FILE IS NOW THE CENTRAL DIPLOMATIC AXIS OF THE CYCLE.** Markets pricing nuclear-energy-security premium as durable: URA +3.7% 1D / +12.3% 1M / +10.6% 3M — outperforming both gold (-0.7% 1M) and oil (+6.3% 1M). **Vali Nasr framework: Iranians want 'meticulously detailed agreement with verification'; Trump wants no detailed document — wants to declare everything solved. The WSJ HEU-with-return-clause IS the meticulous Iranian counter-detail Nasr predicted.** Suzanne Maloney (Brookings): JCPOA 2015 was 159 dense pages — 'very hard to get to anything similar in a matter of days.'

current.iran_heu_concession_status: REPORTED (WSJ primary); REJECTED by Trump publicly May 10-11
current.russia_custodian_status: Standing offer (Putin May 9) UNCONVERTED into named framework; implicit but unnamed candidate
current.iaea_role: Supervision of dilute-portion only per Iranian terms
current.israel_position: Maximalist — Netanyahu CBS 60 Minutes 'war not over until uranium removed and enrichment sites dismantled'

active_triggers.condition: Iran formally accepts leaked Al Arabiya draft text WITHOUT nuclear amendments within 7 days (by 2026-05-29) — confirms asymmetric ceasefire-without-nuclear split
active_triggers.note: Sadjadpour falsifiable test. Acceptance without nuclear amendments = regime accepted the asymmetric split it wanted (ceasefire + sanctions lift, nuclear program preserved). Rejection citing nuclear-track failure = asymmetric split was deliberate Iranian pre-positioning. Insistence on adding nuclear-track separately within 30 days (per 14-point) = also confirms the asymmetric path.

active_triggers.condition: Russia formally named as HEU custodian for Iran transfer under any framework agreement within 30 days (by 2026-06-11)
active_triggers.note_2026-05-28: STAYS watching but probability sharply DOWN. On May 27 Trump explicitly OPPOSED both Russia AND China taking custody of Iran's HEU ('No dust, no dollars'), insisting it 'will be destroyed' in the US, Iran, or 'another acceptable location.' The third-party-custodian path is now affirmatively foreclosed on the US side; the parallel united-states-node 'Russia uranium-custodian framework by 2026-05-18' trigger has RESOLVED untripped. This trigger remains technically open until 2026-06-11 but is now near-dead absent a Trump reversal.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-10: STAYS WATCHING. No custodian framework advanced during the escalation; HEU file static, no movement. (30-day window by 2026-06-11 effectively unfulfilled — Trump opposed both Russia and China custody on 27 May.)

active_triggers.condition: US or Israel strike on Iran enrichment facility (Natanz, Fordow, Arak) — confirmed kinetic
active_triggers.note_2026-06-06: STAYS WATCHING. The 5-6 June US self-defence strikes hit Iranian COASTAL SURVEILLANCE RADAR at Goruk + Qeshm Island — NOT enrichment facilities (Natanz/Fordow/Arak untouched). No kinetic action against the nuclear program.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-10: STAYS WATCHING. Israel's 8-June deep strike hit Tehran/Isfahan/Tabriz/Kermanshah and the Mahshahr PETROCHEMICAL complex (IDF: 'unique materials critical for the ballistic-missile program') — a missile-MATERIALS target, NOT an enrichment facility (Natanz/Fordow/Arak untouched). US 9-June 'warning-shot' strikes hit ~20 coastal air-defense/radar sites, also not enrichment. Threshold not crossed.

active_triggers.condition: Iran withdraws HEU return-clause from any revised counter-proposal — would signal real commitment shift
Iran resumes enrichment to weapons-grade levels (>20% U-235) publicly — signals walk-away from non-proliferation envelope
active_triggers.note_2026-06-06: STAYS WATCHING. IAEA reports a complete loss of continuity and 'movement detected near the stockpile,' but Grossi states Iran 'isn't actively enriching' — no confirmed resumption to >20% or operational policy shift. Negative-space (blackout) persists; no upgrade.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-10: STAYS WATCHING. No enrichment resumption confirmed across the 7-10 June escalation; no walk-back of the reported Mojtaba HEU directive. The deadlock remains money ($24bn frozen assets + Iran's ~$270bn war-damages counter-demand), not enrichment.

top_edges.context: 2026-06-10: Through the war's sharpest escalation the HEU/enrichment file stayed static — no resumption, no Mojtaba-directive walk-back; deadlock remains money not nuclear. Israel hit Mahshahr (missile-MATERIALS, not enrichment). Trump nuclear-letter deadline 15 Jun is 5 days out, unanswered. REPORTED/stated-position — modest.

2026-06-10: Trump's nuclear-letter accept/reject deadline (15 Jun) sits 5 days out with no formal Iranian response (expiry-unanswered). The US 9-June 'warning-shot' strikes hit ~20 air-defense sites, NOT enrichment; HEU file static. Modest, no inflation.

2026-06-10: Israel's 8-June deep strike hit the Mahshahr petrochemical complex — IDF: 'produces unique materials critical for Iran's ballistic-missile program' — the first strike this window touching the missile-MATERIALS supply chain. Not an enrichment facility. CONFIRMED.

2026-06-06: Mojtaba HEU directive un-walked-back, ~5-June window passed quietly; his military adviser Rezaei voices the $24bn deadlock — deadlock now money not nuclear. REPORTED/negative-space — modest.

recent_signals.headline: Enrichment red line restated hard by both sides into the signing: US wants 'zero enrichment' + stockpile removal; Iran's AEOI head rejects enrichment limits and the FM says uranium 'will under no circumstances be transferred anywhere.' The interim MoU (signing Fri) sidesteps this; the 60-day nuclear track inherits an unreconciled red line. CONFIRMED (both public positions).

IAEA Board adopted a resolution (10 Jun) urging Iran to cooperate; IAEA reports it remains unable to verify suspension of enrichment/reprocessing/heavy-water as Iran denies access (ISIS analysis). Deal defers nuclear to a 60-day track; Iran reducing monitoring, not increasing it. No confirmed weapons-grade resumption. REPORTED/CONFIRMED (IAEA institutional).

ENRICHMENT DEFERRED in the framework, but Iran moving the OTHER way. The MoU only contains pledges to FURTHER NEGOTIATE suspension/stockpile removal — not a resolution. Simultaneously Iran's parliament reportedly voted to end AEOI cooperation with the IAEA, and Iran's 10-point counter demands recognition of its sovereign enrichment right. REPORTED. The deal's hardest issue remains unresolved and is trending toward less monitoring, not more.

ENRICHMENT STILL UNRESOLVED — AND NOW A DOMESTIC IRANIAN FIGHT. The US holds 'zero enrichment'; Iran's draft is internally disputed — lawmaker Zohurian called the published text 'irresponsible' for implying enrichment recognition, while some reports say nuclear is DEFERRED to a separate follow-on track beyond the MoU. The near-term deal is signable precisely because it fuzzes/defers the nuclear core. The hardliner rejection is the tell the gap is real, not closed — the single biggest risk the 'final text' doesn't survive a signature.
sources: Iran Intl (Zohurian)
Bloomberg
House of Commons Library
NBC
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED (positions); the contradiction is the open question

recent_signals.headline: THE DEAL'S CENTRAL UNRESOLVED RISK — ENRICHMENT. Reported deal terms have Iran HALTING enrichment for 15-20 years and DISMANTLING nuclear sites (enriched uranium 'destroyed on site and removed from the country'). But Iran's Atomic Energy Organization has publicly REFUSED any limits on enrichment, and talks have been 'deadlocked' on exactly this (US 'zero enrichment' vs Iran 'no limits'). Either Iran has quietly conceded its core red line (genuine breakthrough) or the 'final text' papers over the gap and risks collapsing like ~7 prior soft deadlines.
sources: The Hill
House of Commons Library
CBS/CNBC (reported terms)
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED (each side's stated position); the contradiction is the open question

recent_signals.headline: **ISRAEL'S 8-JUNE DEEP STRIKE TOUCHED THE BALLISTIC-MISSILE MATERIALS SUPPLY CHAIN (MAHSHAHR PETROCHEMICAL, IDF: 'UNIQUE MATERIALS CRITICAL FOR IRAN'S BALLISTIC-MISSILE PROGRAM') — FIRST SUCH STRIKE THIS WINDOW — BUT THE HEU/ENRICHMENT FILE IS UNCHANGED; TRUMP NUCLEAR-LETTER DEADLINE (15 JUN) IS 5 DAYS OUT, UNANSWERED.** Amid the war's sharpest escalation (Iran's first salvo on Israel since the April ceasefire 7 Jun; Israel's deep strike 8 Jun; 24h mutual suspension), the nuclear file did not move: no enrichment resumption confirmed, no walk-back of the reported Mojtaba HEU directive (~440kg 60%-HEU not to leave Iran). Israel's strike on the Mahshahr petrochemical complex is a missile-MATERIALS target (not an enrichment facility) — the first this window to touch the ballistic-missile supply chain. Trump's nuclear-letter accept/reject deadline (15 Jun) sits 5 days out with no formal Iranian response; talks in 'reassessment' -> expiry-unanswered trajectory. No trigger to active (no confirmed enrichment resumption or HEU movement).
sources: IDF statement (Mahshahr petrochemical 'unique materials critical for the ballistic-missile program', 8 Jun)
ISW/Critical Threats Iran Update (Israeli deep strike, 8 Jun)
Reuters / Al Jazeera (Iran 'reassess' negotiations; nuclear-letter deadline 15 Jun carry)
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED (Israeli strike on Mahshahr petrochemical; IDF 'missile-materials' characterization) / REPORTED (Mojtaba HEU directive un-walked-back, single-lineage; nuclear-letter expiry trajectory) — no enrichment resumption, no HEU movement

recent_signals.headline: **HEU-DIRECTIVE WINDOW EXPIRED UN-RESOLVED; NUCLEAR FILE DEFERRED TO RUBIO'S 'PHASE TWO' AS THE IMPASSE MOVES ONTO MONEY.** The reported Mojtaba directive (~440kg 60%-HEU not to leave Iran) held un-walked-back through its 14-day window (by 2026-06-05), which expired with no state-media confirmation and no denial — the thread went dark on schedule. Rubio reframed HEU disposal as a later 'phase two,' consistent with the nuclear-deferred pivot; the core of the impasse is now the $24bn frozen-assets question, not enrichment. IAEA reports a continued complete loss of continuity since 28 Feb (Grossi: not actively enriching, but 'movement detected near the stockpile'). (intel A5)
sources: Reuters/JPost/Haaretz (21 May, carry)
The National (2 Jun, Rubio)
PBS NewsHour (Grossi, carry)
recent_signals.verification: REPORTED (directive single-lineage, window expiry); CONFIRMED (IAEA continued blackout)

recent_signals.headline: **THE DEADLOCK IS NOW MONEY ($24bn), NOT NUCLEAR — HEU DIRECTIVE UN-WALKED-BACK, ~5-JUNE WINDOW PASSED QUIETLY, IAEA REPORTS LOSS OF CONTINUITY + 'MOVEMENT DETECTED NEAR THE STOCKPILE.'** Rezaei (Mojtaba's military adviser) told CNN (5 June) talks are 'at a deadlock' over $24bn in frozen assets ('ball in Trump's court,' doubling from $12bn) — making explicit that the binding sticking-point is now money, not the deferred nuclear file. The reported Mojtaba directive that ~440kg of 60% HEU not leave Iran (~200kg reportedly in the Isfahan tunnel complex) stands un-confirmed and un-walked-back, no proof-of-life, with the internal ~5-June window now PASSED QUIETLY — consistent with internal posture, not an operational act. The IAEA continues to report a complete loss of continuity since the 28-Feb access termination; Grossi says Iran 'isn't actively enriching but movement detected near the stockpile,' urging engagement 'without delay.' Iran's blackout is the operative reality. No trigger moves to active.
sources: CNN 5 June (Rezaei: 'deadlock' over $24bn; nuclear deferred)
Reuters (21 May, two senior Iranian sources) via wire — Mojtaba HEU directive — REPORTED, un-walked-back; Al Jazeera (~440kg / Isfahan tunnel ~200kg)
IAEA / Grossi (loss of continuity since 28 Feb; 'isn't actively enriching but movement detected near stockpile'; engage 'without delay')
Intel dossier 2026-06-06 A2, A5
recent_signals.verification: Deadlock-over-$24bn / nuclear-deferred = stated-position (CONFIRMED Rezaei said it via CNN; substance CLAIMED); Mojtaba HEU directive = REPORTED (single-lineage), un-walked-back, no proof-of-life, ~5-June window passed quietly (CONFIRMED absence); IAEA loss of continuity + 'movement detected near stockpile' + 'engage without delay' = CONFIRMED (IAEA). No confirmed enrichment-resumption or kinetic nuclear action — no trigger to active.

recent_signals.headline: **SUSPENSION HARDENED — NUCLEAR FILE MOVED FURTHER OUT OF REACH; MOJTABA DIRECTIVE ~DAY 13/14, DEADLINE ~48H OUT.** Iran's 3 June hardening (Tasnim: 'no response given on the understanding text,' text-exchange halted until Lebanon conditions met) removes the forum for HEU disposal/custodian arrangements; Rubio countered that there is 'the prospect' Iran could negotiate its nuclear programme (CLAIMED). The Mojtaba directive that ~440kg of 60% HEU not leave Iran (~200kg reportedly in the Isfahan tunnel complex) stands un-confirmed/un-walked-back, ~Day 13 of 14, no proof-of-life. Binary for ~5 June unchanged: confirm or quietly drop the directive. No trigger moves.
sources: Tasnim (via NPR/CNBC) — suspension hardened — Iran position; Rubio Senate testimony (3 June) — US, CLAIMED
Al Jazeera (HEU stockpile ~440kg / Isfahan tunnel ~200kg analysis)
Intel dossier 2026-06-03 A2, A5
recent_signals.verification: Suspension hardening = CONFIRMED Iranian position; Rubio 'prospect of nuclear negotiation' = CLAIMED (US optimistic bound); Mojtaba directive = REPORTED (two senior Iranian sources, one wire), un-walked-back, no proof-of-life. No trigger to active.

recent_signals.headline: **SUSPENSION OF TALKS PUTS NUCLEAR FURTHER OUT OF REACH — FILE KEPT OFF THE TABLE; MOJTABA DIRECTIVE ~DAY 12/14, STILL NO PROOF-OF-LIFE.** Iran's 1 June suspension of the indirect talks (over Israel's Lebanon campaign) removes the forum in which HEU disposal would be negotiated, and IRNA framed this round as 'cessation-of-hostilities only' — nuclear kept off-table. The Mojtaba directive that HEU not leave Iran stands un-confirmed and un-walked-back (~Day 12 of 14, deadline ~5 June); stockpile ~440kg / 970lb at 60% (Al Jazeera). A 2 June aggregation that the 'new supreme leader may be engaged with US talks' is unverified and contradicted by the suspension — NOT elevated. Binary for ~5 June unchanged: confirm or quietly drop the directive. No trigger moves.
sources: Tasnim / Araghchi via ToI/Euronews/CNBC/CNN (suspension; 'all fronts' doctrine; IRNA 'cessation-of-hostilities only')
Al Jazeera — ~440kg/970lb 60% HEU — independent
Reuters (21 May, two senior Iranian sources) via Al Arabiya/CDM — directive — REPORTED, multi-corroborated
Intel dossier 2026-06-02 A1, A5
recent_signals.verification: Talks suspended removing the nuclear-negotiation forum = CONFIRMED (Tasnim/Araghchi); nuclear kept off-table = REPORTED (IRNA framing); Mojtaba HEU directive un-walked-back, ~Day 12/14, no proof-of-life = REPORTED/negative-space; 'new supreme leader engaged' = unverified, NOT elevated.

recent_signals.headline: **IRAN PUBLICLY CLAIMS 'NO NEGOTIATIONS' ON THE NUCLEAR FILE; REPRICES ITS ASK AS $12bn CASH; DESTRUCTION CLAUSE DISPUTED.** On 29 May Iran countered Trump's 'close to finalizing' by claiming no nuclear negotiations are taking place (CLAIMED Iran); Araghchi reaffirmed a deadlock on enriched material ('postponed'). Fars says the draft MOU has NO HEU-destruction clause and that its centerpiece is the immediate $12bn frozen-asset release; Trump insists the HEU 'will be destroyed.' Both are CLAIMED by their respective sides — no signed text. Mojtaba's HEU-stays-in-Iran directive stands un-walked-back; ~Day 9 of 14 (deadline ~June 5), no proof-of-life.
sources: Times of Israel liveblog 29 May (Iran 'no negotiations') — reports Iran's claim
The Tribune / Al Jazeera (Araghchi deadlock at BRICS FM meeting)
Fars via wire summaries (no destruction clause; $12bn-first) — Iran framing, contested
Reuters (Mojtaba directive, two senior sources) — single sourcing cluster
Intel dossier 2026-05-30 A11, A2, B2
recent_signals.verification: Iran 'no negotiations' = CLAIMED Iran (denied-by-implication by US); Araghchi deadlock = REPORTED/CONFIRMED; no-destruction-clause (Iran) and HEU 'will be destroyed' (US) = CLAIMED by each side; Mojtaba directive ~Day 9 of 14 = REPORTED, un-walked-back. No weight elevation on CLAIMED inputs.

recent_signals.headline: **THE NUCLEAR FILE HIT 'DEADLOCK' — THE DISPOSAL-VS-RETENTION CONTRADICTION HARDENED, AND THE THIRD-PARTY-CUSTODIAN PATH (RUSSIA OR CHINA) WAS AFFIRMATIVELY FORECLOSED ON THE US SIDE.** (1) **Araghchi 'deadlock' (REPORTED)**: FM Araghchi said the two sides had reached a 'deadlock' on enriched uranium, with that file 'postponed' to later stages. (2) **Trump hardline (CONFIRMED — PBS, CNBC)**: Iran's ~1,000 lbs HEU 'will be destroyed' — in the US, in Iran, or 'at another acceptable location'; opposes Russia OR China custody ('No dust, no dollars'); 'No sanctions, no money, no nothing.' (3) **Shamkhani 'fantasy' (CONFIRMED RHETORIC)**: again called US control of Iran's nuclear program a 'fantasy.' (4) **White House 'complete fabrication' (CLAIMED vs DENIED — B1)**: dismissed the Iranian-state-TV draft framework (Oman-mediated Hormuz reopening, US withdrawal, blockade lifting). (5) **Tasnim 'generally positive' (CLAIMED, within-regime contradiction)**: Tasnim claimed the Doha nuclear-stockpile talks were 'generally positive' — directly contradicting Araghchi's 'deadlock.' (6) **Mojtaba HEU-retention directive Day 7 of 14** (by June 5): state media still neither confirms nor walks it back; negative-space signal persists. NET: an explicit deadlock plus a foreclosed custodian path; the nuclear file remains the central, and now the most stuck, axis of the deal. **NO upgrade on REPORTED/CLAIMED-CONTESTED inputs; rhetoric/diplomatic posture + negotiating-state report, not confirmed nuclear action.**
sources: GlobalSecurity Day 90 May 28 (Araghchi 'deadlock'; Iran state-TV draft; White House 'complete fabrication')
PBS NewsHour May 27 + CNBC May 27 (Trump HEU 'destroyed'; opposes Russia/China custody)
Tasnim (Doha nuclear-stockpile talks 'generally positive') — Iran-side framing
CNN live May 27 (White House 'complete fabrication')
Intel dossier 2026-05-28 A2, A7, B1
recent_signals.verification: Araghchi 'deadlock' = REPORTED; Trump HEU 'will be destroyed' + opposes Russia/China custody = CONFIRMED (PBS/CNBC on record); Shamkhani 'fantasy' = CONFIRMED RHETORIC; WH 'complete fabrication' of Iran state-TV draft = CONFIRMED (CNN) — the draft itself CLAIMED/DENIED; Tasnim 'generally positive' = CLAIMED (Iran-side, contradicts Araghchi); Mojtaba directive Day 7/14 state-media silence = CONFIRMED ABSENCE.

recent_signals.headline: **THE DEAL REPORTEDLY INCLUDES AN HEU 'DISPOSAL' MECHANISM — YET SHAMKHANI CALLED US CONTROL OF IRAN'S NUCLEAR PROGRAMME A 'FANTASY' AND IRAN WANTS NUCLEAR DEFERRED; MOJTABA HEU-RETENTION DIRECTIVE DAY 6 OF 14, STATE MEDIA STILL SILENT. Trump's 'largely negotiated' deal reportedly contains a mechanism to 'dispose of' Iran's HEU stockpile plus 'strong inspections' — but Khamenei adviser Ali Shamkhani dismissed Trump's desired control over Iran's nuclear programme as a 'fantasy,' Iran insists the nuclear file be deferred, and US reporting (NPR May 27) still lists the nuclear file as one of three deal-blockers alongside sanctions relief and Lebanon-coverage.** (1) **Deal includes HEU 'disposal' mechanism (REPORTED — Axios/CNBC deal text May 24-27)**: 60-day ceasefire extension, Hormuz reopened 'no tolls' + Iran clears mines, US lifts blockade + sanctions waivers, an HEU 'disposal' mechanism, 'strong inspections.' (2) **Shamkhani 'fantasy' (CONFIRMED RHETORIC)**: Khamenei adviser Ali Shamkhani called US control over Iran's nuclear programme a 'fantasy' — the genuinely new hard edge on the nuclear clause, though aimed at the clause, not at the talks themselves. (3) **Mojtaba HEU-retention directive Day 6 of 14** (toward June 5): NO Iranian state-media outlet (IRNA, Tasnim, Mehr, Press TV) has confirmed, denied, or walked back the May 21 leaked directive — the negative-space signal persists, now in direct tension with the reportedly-agreed 'disposal' mechanism. (4) **Nuclear remains a named deal-blocker (CONFIRMED — NPR May 27)**: the finalisation is held up by language on the nuclear programme and sanctions relief, plus the Lebanon-coverage dispute. **The three-way contradiction is unchanged**: admin deal-optimist (disposal agreed) vs Iran nuclear-preservation (deferred / 'fantasy' on control, directive un-walked) vs Trump-Netanyahu private maximalist (dismantlement required). **Falsifiable**: a published MOU within 14 days resolves which framing is in the text; explicit Iranian state-media confirmation OR walk-back of the Mojtaba directive by June 5 falsifies one framing. **NO upgrade on REPORTED/CLAIMED-CONTESTED inputs; today is rhetoric/diplomatic posture, not confirmed nuclear action.**
sources: Axios May 24-26 Barak Ravid (deal text incl. HEU 'disposal' mechanism + 'strong inspections')
CNBC May 26-27 'Trump says Iran deal... largely negotiated'
NPR May 27 (deal held up by nuclear/sanctions/Lebanon; Camp David Cabinet)
Intel dossier 2026-05-27 A5 (Shamkhani 'fantasy'; nuclear deferred), A8
NO IRNA/Tasnim/Mehr/Press TV original Mojtaba HEU-directive confirmation or walk-back through May 27 (CONFIRMED ABSENCE)
recent_signals.verification: Deal includes HEU 'disposal' mechanism + 'strong inspections' = REPORTED (Axios/CNBC deal text) / CONTESTED contents; Shamkhani 'fantasy' on US nuclear control = CONFIRMED RHETORIC; Iran wants nuclear deferred = CONFIRMED RHETORIC (Iran-side); nuclear as one of three named deal-blockers = CONFIRMED (US reporting convergence, NPR); Mojtaba HEU-retention directive Day 6 with no state-media confirm/walk-back = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (negative-space). No confirmed nuclear action — no upgrade.

recent_signals.headline: **DISPOSAL-VS-'NOT IN TALKS' CONTRADICTION PERSISTS; MOJTABA HEU DIRECTIVE DAY 4 OF 14 — STILL NO STATE-MEDIA CONFIRMATION OR WALK-BACK. US SOURCES (AXIOS) SAY IRANIANS GAVE ASSURANCES 'VERBAL AND IN WRITING' THAT ANY PERMANENT DEAL WILL INCLUDE 'DISPOSAL' OF ALL ENRICHED URANIUM AND THAT MOJTABA 'ENDORSED THE BROAD TEMPLATE'; IRAN FM BAGHAEI: NUCLEAR 'ISN'T PART OF DISCUSSIONS AT THIS STAGE.' PEZESHKIAN: IRAN 'IS READY TO ASSURE THE WORLD IT IS NOT PURSUING NUCLEAR WEAPONS' — RESOLVES NOTHING. DELEGATION IN DOHA WORKING THE HEU TRACK WITH QATAR'S PM.** (1) **US disposal-assurance claim (REPORTED — Axios May 24, single channel; Pravda-USA aggregating a US official, CBS)**: the Iranians 'gave assurances — verbal and in writing — that any subsequent permanent deal will include the 'disposal' of all enriched uranium, higher and lower levels,' and Mojtaba Khamenei has 'endorsed the broad template' though final approval is open. (2) **Iran position (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — Baghaei via NBC News / CNBC / IRNA / Press TV / Mehr, May 25)**: the nuclear programme 'isn't part of discussions at this stage' — the cleanest Iranian counter to the US disposal-agreement claim. (3) **Mojtaba HEU-retention directive Day 4 of 14** (toward June 5): NO Iranian state-media outlet (IRNA, Tasnim, Mehr, Press TV) has confirmed, denied, or walked back the May 21 leaked directive; the wire chain continues to recycle the original Reuters anonymous-source piece. **The negative-space signal persists.** (4) **Pezeshkian no-weapons assurance (CONFIRMED RHETORIC)**: Iran 'is ready to assure the world it is not pursuing nuclear weapons' — a non-proliferation assurance rhetorically compatible with BOTH keeping and disposing of the stockpile; it does not resolve the disposal-vs-retention contradiction. (5) **HEU now has a physical venue (CONFIRMED ACTION, context)**: a senior Iranian delegation (Speaker Ghalibaf, FM Araghchi, central-bank governor Hemmati) arrived in Doha May 25 for talks 'related to the Strait of Hormuz and highly enriched uranium' plus frozen-asset unfreezing, meeting Qatar's PM — the HEU workstream is in-venue but no published text. (6) **Trump-Netanyahu private maximalist line (REPORTED — TOI sources, carried forward)**: Trump 'will not sign any final agreement without' Iran dismantling its entire nuclear program and removing all enriched uranium — contradicts the admin's public 'in principle agreed to disposal' framing. **The three-way contradiction is unchanged**: admin deal-optimist (disposal agreed, verbal+written) vs Iran nuclear-preservation (not in current talks, directive un-walked) vs Trump-Netanyahu private maximalist (dismantlement required). **Falsifiable**: a published MOU within 14 days resolves which framing is in the text; explicit Iranian state-media confirmation OR walk-back of the Mojtaba directive by June 5 falsifies one framing. **Sadjadpour asymmetric-split thesis at maximum institutional expression** — Iran appears to be getting the ceasefire/Hormuz/sanctions-without-uranium-dismantlement split it wanted, but only inside the deferred-window architecture. **NO upgrade on REPORTED/CLAIMED-CONTESTED inputs; today is rhetoric/diplomatic posture, not confirmed nuclear action.**
sources: Axios May 24 Barak Ravid (HEU 'verbal and in writing' disposal-assurance claim + Mojtaba 'endorsed broad template')
CBS News May 24-25 liveblog (broad-principles sourcing)

Times of Israel May 25 (Pezeshkian 'ready to assure the world not pursuing nuclear weapons'; Trump-Netanyahu private dismantlement assurance carryover)
Euronews / Daily Sabah / Press TV May 25 (Doha delegation HEU agenda)
NO IRNA/Tasnim/Mehr/Press TV original Mojtaba HEU-directive confirmation or walk-back May 22-25 (CONFIRMED ABSENCE)

No confirmed nuclear action — no upgrade.

recent_signals.headline: **MOJTABA KHAMENEI'S FIRST POLICY DIRECTIVE LEAKED MAY 21 — URANIUM ENRICHED TO 60% TO 'REMAIN STRICTLY INSIDE IRANIAN TERRITORY.' DIRECTLY FORECLOSES TRUMP'S MAY 16 LETTER NUCLEAR BASIS. PAKISTAN-MEDIATED DRAFT TEXT (LEAKED MAY 22) ADDRESSES HORMUZ/CEASEFIRE/SANCTIONS BUT OMITS URANIUM — DEAL ARCHITECTURE BIFURCATED.** Anonymous Iranian sources told Reuters (propagated via Algemeiner, Kathmandu Post, CDM Press May 21) that 'Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei ordered that Iran's stockpile of uranium enriched to 60% remain strictly inside Iranian territory' and that 'The Supreme Leader's directive, and the consensus within the establishment, is that the stockpile of enriched uranium should not leave the country.' Reasoning per sources: Iran would be 'more vulnerable to future attacks by the United States and Israel' if material left. **Structurally the first publicly-attributed substantive policy directive from Mojtaba since his March appointment, surfaced AT THE EXACT MOMENT the Pakistan-mediated framework is moving** — timing is regime-internal signal to Trump that the nuclear track is foreclosed independent of operational deliverable architecture. **The Al Arabiya leaked draft (May 22)** addresses Hormuz freedom-of-navigation + ceasefire + sanctions phasing + talks within 7 days — but NOT uranium dismantlement. **The deal architecture has bifurcated**: ceasefire/Hormuz/sanctions deliverable executable in days via Pakistan; nuclear deliverable foreclosed by Mojtaba's directive. **Sadjadpour falsifiable**: deal publicly announced within 7 days addressing Hormuz/ceasefire/sanctions WITHOUT uranium dismantlement = regime accepted asymmetric split. Trump walks away citing nuclear-track failure = asymmetric split was deliberate. Iran softens directive within 14 days = directive was bargaining noise. **Verification**: Reuters wire amplification via 3 outlets all trace to anonymous Iranian sources — counts as ONE source per protocol. Tag at REPORTED, not CONFIRMED. **Issacharoff thesis (May 18) strengthened**: Iran's nuclear infrastructure intact underground; Tehran is signaling it has NOT, and will NOT, convert nuclear program into deal currency. **Rubio's May 21 Hormuz red line** sets second failure mode parallel to nuclear-track failure mode — even if Iran walks back uranium directive, Iran-Oman toll mechanism is separate publicly-stated US-acceptability test.
sources: CDM Press May 21 'Ayatollah Orders Highly-Enriched Uranium To Remain In Iran' (Reuters wire)
Kathmandu Post May 21 (Reuters wire)
Algemeiner May 21 (Reuters wire)
Al Arabiya English May 22 (Pakistan-mediated final draft text)
Reuters citing anonymous Iranian sources (single primary chain)
CBS News May 21 (Rubio Hormuz red line)
Intel dossier 2026-05-22 A4, B1, B2, B6
recent_signals.verification: Mojtaba uranium directive = REPORTED (Reuters wire via 3 outlets trace to ONE primary source per protocol); Pakistan-mediated draft text = REPORTED (Al Arabiya primary leak; ILNA/Tasnim trace to same chain); deal architecture bifurcation = INFERRED structural reading based on REPORTED inputs; Rubio Hormuz red line = CONFIRMED RHETORIC (CBS primary); Issacharoff thesis = remains REPORTED (Haaretz May 18 sourcing).

recent_signals.headline: **IRAN COUNTER-PROPOSAL VIA PAKISTAN OPERATIONALIZES FAR-APART POSITIONING ON ENRICHMENT — BAGHAEI ENUMERATED IRAN'S DEMANDS (asset unfreezing + sanctions lift + port-blockade end + Lebanon ceasefire + war-damage compensation) WITH NO CONCESSION ON ENRICHMENT.** This is the formal articulation of the gap with Trump's May 16 letter terms (full dismantling + 2-month compliance + zero enrichment red line). **The Issacharoff thesis (Haaretz May 18) is operationally re-tested**: Iran's nuclear infrastructure and missile production capabilities remain largely intact underground per former senior Israeli intelligence officer; Tehran may now conclude only nuclear weapons can deter future wars. Today's Iranian counter-proposal does NOT include enrichment concession, which is consistent with the Issacharoff thesis (Iran more, not less, likely to weaponize). **Trump's postponement explicitly frames the kinetic option as paused not cancelled** — every additional day of postponement is a day Iran's underground infrastructure remains operational, which is the Issacharoff-implied Israeli operational concern.
sources: Tasnim (Baghaei counter-proposal)
Trump Truth Social (postponement post)
Haaretz May 18 (Issacharoff frame carries forward)
Intel dossier 2026-05-19 A1, A5
recent_signals.verification: Baghaei counter-proposal demands = CONFIRMED RHETORIC (Tasnim primary); no concession on enrichment = CONFIRMED ABSENCE in counter-proposal; Issacharoff thesis still REPORTED (Haaretz piece sourcing).

recent_signals.headline: **NYT REPORTS 'MOST INTENSE PREPARATIONS YET' FOR RENEWED US+ISRAELI STRIKES ON IRANIAN NUCLEAR INFRASTRUCTURE 'AS SOON AS NEXT WEEK' — INCLUDES COMMANDO OPERATION TO EXTRACT NUCLEAR MATERIAL FROM STRUCK SITES.** NYT scoop (Friday May 15 / Saturday May 16, citing two Middle Eastern officials) detailed two options: **(i) commando operation to extract nuclear material believed buried under rubble at struck nuclear sites** — 'thousands of supporting forces to create a perimeter,' US officials acknowledged the operation 'would risk several casualties' and likely engage Iranian ground troops; **(ii) ground forces to seize Kharg Island** (handles up to 90% of Iranian oil exports — note: an oil-export asset, NOT a nuclear site, but pressure point on nuclear-file leverage). Amplified by Times of Israel May 16 liveblog, Jerusalem Post 'US-Israel Iran attacks could resume next week' (article 896353), Gulf News 'Operation Epic Fury 2.0,' Fox News 'Next US move on Iran: Seize Kharg Island.' **NO on-record Pentagon/White House confirmation of operation planning.** Iran has REPORTEDLY (single-source US intel chain via NYT) 'laid traps and moved additional military personnel and air defenses to Kharg Island in recent weeks' in preparation for possible US operation. Operational signature can be cross-checked in coming days. **The nuclear file is now under direct kinetic-preparation pressure, not just rhetorical pressure** — operationally distinct from any prior episode in the cycle. Trump 'Calm Before the Storm' Truth Social post + warship-Iran animation (CONFIRMED RHETORIC) provide the rhetorical envelope. **PAKISTAN-NAQVI MEDIATION CHANNEL OPERATIONALLY ACTIVE IN PARALLEL** (CONFIRMED ACTION) — Pakistani Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi arrived Tehran Saturday May 16 on 2-day official visit explicitly described as 'to facilitate' US-Iran peace talks; received by Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni. The simultaneity is the signal: kinetic-preparation track and Pakistan-mediation track running simultaneously. **Sadjadpour falsifiable**: if US strikes commence within 7 days (by May 24), 'rhetoric without action' leg fails. **Nasr structural-mismatch thesis at sharpest of cycle**: Trump's 2-month timeline compresses while Iran's six-pillar architecture stretches.
sources: New York Times Friday-Saturday May 15-16 via Times of Israel May 16 liveblog amplification — two Middle Eastern officials attribution (national security desk)
Times of Israel May 16 liveblog 'As Iran talks stall, Israel and US prepping to renew war as soon as next week — report'
Jerusalem Post May 16 article 896353 'US-Israel Iran attacks could resume next week'
Gulf News May 17 'Operation Epic Fury 2.0: US and Israel Weigh Kharg Island Strike, Uranium Seizure Mission'
Fox News 'Next US move on Iran: Seize Kharg Island'
Trump Truth Social primary (Calm Before the Storm + warship-Iran animation)
Daily Pakistan + Pakistan Today + BSS News (Naqvi-Tehran visit primary)
recent_signals.verification: NYT preparation report (commando + Kharg ground option + 'as soon as next week') = REPORTED only (two Middle Eastern officials, NYT national security desk; multi-outlet amplification; NO on-record Pentagon/WH confirmation); Iran-Kharg air-defense reinforcement = REPORTED (single-source US intel chain via NYT); Trump Truth Social posts = CONFIRMED RHETORIC (Trump primary); Pakistan-Naqvi Tehran visit = CONFIRMED ACTION (multi-source primary).

recent_signals.headline: A12 IRAN TONE HARDENING ON NUCLEAR FILE — Iranian Parliament NSFP Commission spokesperson Ebrahim Rezaei publicly named 90% enrichment as retaliatory threshold if Iran is struck again (CONFIRMED RHETORIC, NOT ACTION); first time in cycle a sitting Iranian parliamentary spokesperson has publicly named weapons-grade enrichment as a tripwire. Current stated ceiling 60% with ~440 kg stockpile; 90% = breakout threshold. Mehr News + Tabnak + Euronews + Iran International + Tasnim + Al Bawaba all amplified without editorial softening = Iranian state-media system permitted weapons-grade rhetoric as sanctioned policy signal, not leaked outlier. Tasnim on-record source: 'No one in Iran drafts plans to please Trump.' IRNA + Tasnim + Parliament now uniformly defiant (vs April's IRNA-Tasnim divergence pattern). The 90% rhetoric REFRAMES yesterday's HEU-with-return-clause concession: Parliament walking up escalation ladder while executive offers custody flexibility through Pakistan. Iranian Deputy Labour Minister Mohammadi publicly admitted >1 million direct job losses + 2 million unemployment-impacted — Tehran publicly admitting domestic economic deterioration AND publicly hardening on nuclear file simultaneously = classical regime-survival externalisation playbook. NO US (Trump/WH) response to Rezaei threat as of May 13 morning. Netanyahu 60 Minutes Sunday 'go in and take it out' structurally aligned with Israeli hawk camp. URA -5.0% 1D (first material correction of cleanest decoupled long thesis) = market trimming on RHETORIC vs ACTION distinction; no fresh HEU news, no Russia custodian announcement (Day 14 of 21 post-Putin-Araghchi window) = catalyst gap.
sources: Mehr News Agency (Iranian state)
Tabnak (Iranian state)
Euronews 'Iran threatens 90% enrichment as talks falter'
Iran International (opposition-leaning, factual on quote)
Tasnim News Agency (IRGC-linked)
Al Bawaba 'Iran warns weapons-grade enrichment'
Jerusalem Post citing NYT '2 million jobs lost'
CBS 60 Minutes (Netanyahu, prior)
yfinance (URA $54.35 -5.0% 1D)
recent_signals.verification: Rezaei 90% enrichment statement = CONFIRMED RHETORIC NOT ACTION (Iran state media + opposition + Western press) — conditional on future attack, NOT enrichment-policy change; Tasnim 'no one drafts plans to please Trump' = CONFIRMED Iranian-state primary; IRNA/Tasnim/Parliament synchronisation = CONFIRMED multi-channel; Mohammadi labour-ministry job-loss admission = CONFIRMED (NYT/JPost relay); URA -5.0% = CONFIRMED tape (yfinance) — positioning unwind, not thesis falsification.

recent_signals.headline: IRAN'S MAY 10-11 COUNTER-PROPOSAL CONTAINED FIRST HEU CONCESSION OF CYCLE — dilute portion under IAEA + transfer remainder to third country, but WITH EXPLICIT RETURN CLAUSE demanding HEU returnability if US exits future deal AND explicitly REJECTING dismantling of enrichment facilities (REPORTED — WSJ primary). Russia (Putin May 9) implicit candidate but unnamed. **TRUMP REJECTED PUBLICLY ('TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE' / 'garbage' / '1% chance of living').** Netanyahu CBS 60 Minutes May 11: war 'not over' until uranium removed + enrichment sites dismantled — 'you go in and you take it out.' New OFAC sanctions May 12 targeted Iranian nuclear research with military applications. **THE NUCLEAR FILE IS NOW THE CENTRAL DIPLOMATIC AXIS OF THE CYCLE.** URA +3.7% 1D / +12.3% 1M = markets pricing nuclear-energy-security premium as durable. Nasr framework: Iranians want 'meticulously detailed agreement with verification'; Trump wants no detailed document. WSJ HEU-with-return-clause IS the meticulous Iranian counter-detail. Maloney: JCPOA 2015 was 159 dense pages — very hard in matter of days.
sources: WSJ primary (Iran 14-point HEU terms)
Bloomberg + Reuters + IRNA + Tasnim + Press TV + Al Jazeera + NPR (amplification)
CBS 60 Minutes (Netanyahu)
Truth Social + Washington Post (Trump rejection)
Treasury primary (OFAC May 11-12 nuclear-research designations)
Bloomberg Vali Nasr interview
Brookings Suzanne Maloney 'The Third Islamic Republic'
Pravda EN (Russia uranium offer)
recent_signals.verification: Iran HEU concession with return clause = REPORTED (WSJ primary single-source on full terms; Iran-side separately corroborated via IRNA/Tasnim; amplification cascade real); Trump rejection = CONFIRMED multi-source primary; Netanyahu 60 Minutes statements = CONFIRMED RHETORIC (broadcast primary); OFAC nuclear-research designations May 12 = CONFIRMED (Treasury primary); Russia custodian path = REPORTED (Pravda + Iran International) but unnamed in Iranian counter-proposal
